response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,19]],"date-time":"2026-05-19T16:09:14Z","timestamp":1779206954534,"version":"3.51.4"},"reference-count":62,"publisher":"MDPI AG","issue":"10","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,19]],"date-time":"2026-05-19T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1779148800000},"content-version":"vor","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"https:\/\/creativecommons.org\/licenses\/by\/4.0\/"}],"funder":[{"name":"Department of Science and Innovation and the Centre for Scientific and Industrial Research (IBS) Programme, South Africa","award":["201909607"],"award-info":[{"award-number":["201909607"]}]}],"content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":["Diagnostics"],"abstract":"<jats:p>Background\/Objectives: Data augmentation is a foundational component of modern deep learning for enhancing robustness and generalization. However, medical imaging lacks a universally reliable augmentation strategy, forcing researchers into an inefficient \u201caugmentation lottery\u201d that hinders experimental progress and reproducibility. To address this challenge, we introduce Stepwise Upper and Lower Boundaries Augmentation (SULBA), a simple, parameter-free framework designed to eliminate per-task augmentation tuning. Methods: SULBA generates training variations through stepwise cyclic shifts applied along data dimensions, making it inherently applicable to 2D, 3D, and higher-dimensional medical imaging data. To evaluate the efficacy of SULBA as a default DA strategy, we performed benchmarking across 27 publicly available datasets spanning classification and segmentation tasks and 10 convolutional and transformer-based architectures using standard deep learning performance metrics. Results: The results demonstrate that SULBA achieves the highest overall performance and consistently outperforms 16 widely used standard augmentation techniques while delivering robust and reliable improvements without task- or parameter-specific tuning Conclusions: SULBA establishes a principled universal default for data augmentation in medical imaging, with the potential to accelerate the development of generalizable and reproducible medical AI systems.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.3390\/diagnostics16101546","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,19]],"date-time":"2026-05-19T15:17:23Z","timestamp":1779203843000},"page":"1546","update-policy":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.3390\/mdpi_crossmark_policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["SULBA: A Task-Agnostic Data Augmentation Framework for Deep Learning in Medical Image Analysis"],"prefix":"10.3390","volume":"16","author":[{"ORCID":"https:\/\/orcid.org\/0000-0002-5348-5270","authenticated-orcid":false,"given":"Ayomide Adeyemi","family":"Abe","sequence":"first","affiliation":[{"name":"Department of Medical Physics, School of Medicine, Sefako Makgatho Health Sciences University, Pretoria 0028, South Africa"},{"name":"AureXida Inc., Toronto, ON M4M 1Y3, Canada"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Mpumelelo","family":"Nyathi","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[{"name":"Department of Medical Physics, School of Medicine, Sefako Makgatho Health Sciences University, Pretoria 0028, South Africa"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"1968","published-online":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,19]]},"reference":[{"key":"ref_1","doi-asserted-by":"crossref","first-page":"1309","DOI":"10.1038\/s41591-024-02915-w","article-title":"Prediction of tumor origin in cancers of unknown primary origin with cytology-based deep learning","volume":"30","author":"Tian","year":"2024","journal-title":"Nat.
